top_signals.title: Saudi Arabia Crude Oil Exports Hit Lowest on Record in April
top_signals.summary: Crude oil exports from Saudi Arabia fell for a second straight month in April to a record low, according to Joint Organizations Data Initiative (JODI) data released on Monday, as the Iran war disrupted shipments from the Gulf region.Exports…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Treasuries Holds Losses After Oil Drops on US-Iran Progress
top_signals.summary: Treasuries fell as trading resumed following a US public holiday, with Washington and Tehran working to hammer out details of a peace accord.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil Prices Fall 4% as US-Iran Talks Ease Supply Risks
top_signals.summary: Oil prices fell 4% on Monday after U.S. Vice President JD Vance said progress has been made in talks with Iran and that the Strait of Hormuz was open, easing supply concerns.Brent crude was down $3.18 or 3.95%, at $77.39 a barrel by 11:44 a.m.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Yen jumps on reports of Katayama-Bessent talks
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Espriella’s Narrow Win Caps Post Election Gains in Colombia
top_signals.summary: The rally sparked by Abelardo de la Espriella’s win in Colombian elections faded just hours into the session as investors worried that one of the tightest presidential elections in the nation’s history will limit his mandate to implement market-friendly policies.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
